delay_risks: Delays in securing reliable Arctic shipping routes due to unpredictable ice conditions or severe winter storms could delay the entire supply chain setup. Additionally, potential delays from the Danish supplier's internal approval processes or production scheduling may impact contract finalization timelines.
mitigation_strategy: Proactively engage with the Danish supplier early to align on delivery windows and include contingency clauses for weather-related delays. Simultaneously, initiate parallel discussions with Nordic Cargo for emergency air freight to ensure rapid response if sea shipments are disrupted. Conduct a pre-season risk assessment with maritime experts to anticipate seasonal bottlenecks.

delay_risks: Uncertainty in Arctic shipping schedules due to ice blockages or extreme weather events could delay the initial clay shipment. Additionally, potential misalignment between the supplier’s production cycle and our project timeline may result in missed delivery windows.
mitigation_strategy: Establish a formalized communication protocol with the supplier including weekly status updates and real-time tracking integration. Include a clause in the contract allowing for rescheduling without penalty due to Arctic weather disruptions. Begin the process well ahead of the first shipment to allow buffer time for adjustments.

delay_risks: The Danish supplier may face internal delays in finalizing the contract terms, especially if legal or compliance reviews are required. Additionally, last-minute changes in pricing or logistics terms could prolong negotiations.
mitigation_strategy: Assign a dedicated project liaison to manage contract negotiations with the supplier, ensuring all documentation is prepared in advance. Use a standardized contract template aligned with Greenlandic and Danish legal requirements to reduce back-and-forth. Set a firm deadline for finalization and include escalation protocols with senior management if progress stalls.

delay_risks: Delays in securing emergency air freight due to Arctic weather disruptions or limited availability of cargo capacity during peak winter months, which could delay the delivery of critical clay supplies.
mitigation_strategy: Pre-negotiate and lock in a dedicated emergency air freight slot with Nordic Cargo during high-risk periods; maintain a buffer stock of 50 kg clay to bridge any potential gaps in supply chain continuity.

delay_risks: Potential delays in receiving or processing clay shipments from Denmark due to customs clearance issues, shipping schedule changes, or unforeseen logistical bottlenecks in the Arctic maritime corridor.
mitigation_strategy: Coordinate closely with the Danish supplier and Nordic Cargo to align shipment schedules with pre-approved customs documentation; implement real-time tracking via the monitoring system to trigger early alerts and initiate contingency plans if delays exceed 48 hours.

delay_risks: Integration delays with the IoT monitoring system due to technical incompatibility, lack of on-site connectivity, or insufficient training for staff to interpret sensor data effectively.
mitigation_strategy: Conduct a pilot integration phase with the sensor platform before full deployment; provide hands-on training sessions for all staff and include a dedicated support contact for troubleshooting; establish a feedback loop to refine alert thresholds based on real-world usage.

delay_risks: Delays in obtaining accurate thermal data due to limited access to Arctic-specific building simulation tools or delays in securing a certified Arctic building specialist for the audit. Unforeseen structural issues during the pre-fit-out phase could also extend timeline.
mitigation_strategy: Engage a certified Arctic building specialist early in the process and conduct preliminary simulations using historical climate data. Schedule buffer time in the project timeline for unexpected structural findings, and maintain open communication with the Nuuk Municipality planning department to preempt regulatory delays.

delay_risks: Inaccurate input parameters for the EnergyPlus model due to incomplete or outdated local weather data, or delays in receiving approval from the certified Arctic specialist. Software configuration errors or lack of expertise in running complex simulations could also slow progress.
mitigation_strategy: Use verified Greenlandic meteorological data from the Danish Meteorological Institute and validate all inputs with the Arctic building specialist. Assign a trained energy modeling technician to lead the simulation, and conduct peer review of results before finalizing the thermal load assessment.

delay_risks: Delays in sourcing or installing south-facing windows and thermal mass materials due to supply chain constraints or shipping delays from Denmark. Weather-related disruptions during installation (e.g., snowstorms) could also impact on-site work.
mitigation_strategy: Order materials well in advance with a confirmed delivery window; use Nordic Cargo’s emergency air freight agreement as backup. Schedule installation during the most favorable weather window (late spring/early summer), and include a 5-day buffer for weather-related delays. Conduct pre-installation site checks to ensure structural readiness.

delay_risks: Delays in sensor delivery due to Arctic shipping disruptions or customs clearance issues; potential technical integration challenges with the existing data platform; unexpected site conditions requiring additional wiring or mounting solutions.
mitigation_strategy: Pre-order sensors from a local Nordic supplier with guaranteed delivery timelines; conduct pre-installation testing in a controlled environment; assign a dedicated technician for on-site installation and real-time troubleshooting; maintain backup sensor units for immediate replacement if needed.

delay_risks: Unavailability of certified Arctic building specialist due to scheduling conflicts or travel restrictions during winter months; delays in gathering required documentation from contractors; low instructor engagement leading to incomplete training sessions.
mitigation_strategy: Secure the specialist’s availability in writing at least 6 weeks in advance; establish a digital document repository for real-time access and tracking; mandate attendance via the digital shift calendar and follow up with non-participants through the safety officer; conduct training in two staggered sessions to ensure full coverage.

delay_risks: Resistance from staff or users to adhere to curfew rules; lack of clear communication about enforcement procedures; failure to integrate the protocol into daily operations without disrupting course schedules.
mitigation_strategy: Co-develop the curfew policy with instructors and volunteers during a joint workshop; roll out the protocol with a phased pilot phase using real-time sensor alerts; embed reminders into the digital shift calendar and display bilingual signage at key entry points; conduct a brief orientation session before each winter session to reinforce compliance.

delay_risks: Instructor availability may be impacted by seasonal migration, personal commitments, or unexpected absences. Cross-training delays could occur if key instructors are unavailable for training sessions, leading to gaps in coverage during peak program periods.
mitigation_strategy: Implement a rotating instructor schedule with built-in overlap periods and mandatory cross-training modules. Require all instructors to complete training within two weeks of onboarding and use the digital shift calendar to flag potential conflicts early. Conduct monthly safety drills to reinforce team cohesion and readiness.

delay_risks: Delays may arise from scheduling conflicts among part-time instructors due to competing personal or professional obligations. Incomplete cross-training could result in insufficient backup capacity, increasing risk of session cancellations during staff absences.
mitigation_strategy: Use the digital shift calendar to coordinate schedules in advance and require all instructors to confirm availability at least 30 days ahead. Mandate that each instructor completes cross-training in at least two core skill areas (hand-building, wheel-throwing) before being approved for independent teaching. Track progress via the real-time dashboard and hold bi-weekly check-ins to address bottlenecks.

delay_risks: Mentorship effectiveness may be hindered by mismatched availability, lack of engagement from junior instructors, or insufficient guidance from senior mentors. Delays in establishing trust or clear expectations could slow down knowledge transfer and reduce program impact.
mitigation_strategy: Structure mentorship pairs based on complementary skills and availability using the digital shift calendar. Set clear goals and milestones for each mentoring cycle (e.g., one skill mastered per month). Provide a standardized mentorship toolkit with discussion guides and feedback forms, and conduct quarterly evaluations to ensure accountability and continuous improvement.

delay_risks: Volunteers may be hesitant to participate due to lack of recognition or unclear roles; potential delays in onboarding if community members are unavailable for training sessions.
mitigation_strategy: Develop a formal fellowship charter with clear roles, benefits (e.g., public recognition, skill credits), and structured onboarding; engage local cultural leaders to co-promote the program and ensure inclusive recruitment.

delay_risks: Integration issues with existing scheduling tools or data sync failures between staff devices; delays if technical support is unavailable during setup.
mitigation_strategy: Use a cloud-based platform with proven Arctic reliability (e.g., Google Workspace + custom dashboard via Airtable); conduct a pilot test with one instructor before full rollout and assign a dedicated tech liaison for troubleshooting.

delay_risks: Staff availability conflicts due to seasonal workloads or personal commitments; drills may be skipped if not prioritized or if emergency protocols are unclear.
mitigation_strategy: Schedule drills at consistent times (e.g., first Friday of each month) and integrate them into the rotating instructor schedule; use bilingual safety briefings and include drill participation in performance reviews to reinforce accountability.

delay_risks: Delays in sourcing locally available minerals due to remote extraction challenges or environmental restrictions; potential inconsistencies in clay composition affecting firing performance; resistance from local artisans to co-creation processes may slow down trial iterations.
mitigation_strategy: Partner with Greenlandic geologists and indigenous knowledge holders early to identify viable mineral sources; conduct trials in controlled batches with real-time feedback loops; establish clear co-creation agreements and cultural protocols to ensure trust and timely collaboration.

delay_risks: Delays in securing participation from elders and artists due to scheduling conflicts, language barriers, or lack of trust in institutional processes; potential for misalignment in cultural values leading to prolonged consensus-building.
mitigation_strategy: Engage community liaisons familiar with local networks to facilitate introductions; schedule meetings during culturally appropriate times (e.g., after traditional gatherings); provide bilingual materials and offer honorariums to acknowledge time and expertise, ensuring inclusive and respectful engagement.

delay_risks: Legal or cultural disagreements over ownership of traditional motifs and designs may delay finalization; complex approval processes involving multiple stakeholders could extend timelines beyond expectations.
mitigation_strategy: Co-develop the CIPR framework through participatory workshops with elders, artists, and legal advisors; use a phased approval model where initial drafts are reviewed by smaller subgroups before broader consultation; maintain transparent documentation and digital records of all consent decisions.

delay_risks: Potential delays in scheduling due to conflicting availability of Katuaq Cultural Centre’s exhibition calendar, last-minute changes in curatorial direction, or lack of alignment on cultural representation in shared exhibitions.
mitigation_strategy: Establish a joint planning committee with Katuaq Cultural Centre early in the process; schedule quarterly coordination meetings at least two months in advance; co-develop exhibition themes and timelines in writing with mutual sign-off to ensure alignment and reduce last-minute revisions.

delay_risks: Delays may occur if elders or artists are unavailable for consultation, if there is misalignment between traditional content and beginner-level pedagogy, or if cultural sensitivities lead to revisions after initial drafts.
mitigation_strategy: Engage elders and artists through formal advisory council meetings with scheduled input windows; develop draft course modules for review with the council before finalization; incorporate feedback loops with clear decision-making protocols to avoid prolonged revision cycles.

delay_risks: Delays may arise from translation inaccuracies, lack of qualified bilingual staff, or approval bottlenecks from community reviewers, especially if cultural nuance is not properly preserved.
mitigation_strategy: Partner with a certified Greenlandic language specialist and a local cultural liaison to oversee all translations; implement a dual-review process involving both linguistic accuracy and cultural appropriateness; use a digital workflow with version tracking and stakeholder approval logs to streamline turnaround time.

delay_risks: Delays in securing community consensus or intellectual property rights from elders and artists may stall material co-creation efforts. Additionally, technical challenges in analyzing blended clays could extend testing timelines due to limited local expertise or equipment access.
mitigation_strategy: Engage the advisory council early through structured workshops to build trust and clarify expectations; conduct preliminary clay analysis with a Danish ceramics lab before on-site trials to reduce uncertainty; allocate buffer time for iterative feedback cycles with local artisans.

delay_risks: Local mineral sourcing may be delayed due to logistical constraints in remote Greenlandic regions, including weather disruptions or lack of available samples. Resistance from community members to using unfamiliar materials could also slow down trial progress.
mitigation_strategy: Partner with local geologists and mining cooperatives to identify accessible mineral sources early; conduct initial trials with small-scale samples via air freight; involve community leaders in co-design sessions to ensure cultural relevance and foster ownership.

delay_risks: Incompatibility between locally sourced minerals and standard firing temperatures may require multiple retests, delaying final approval. Limited access to kiln testing facilities or inconsistent firing results due to Arctic climate fluctuations could further impact reliability.
mitigation_strategy: Establish a controlled test environment with calibrated kilns and real-time monitoring; collaborate with Køge Keramik to benchmark results against known standards; implement a phased testing protocol with interim review checkpoints to catch issues early.

delay_risks: Delays in obtaining locally sourced minerals due to unpredictable weather conditions or logistical challenges in remote areas of Greenland; potential resistance from local artisans if co-creation processes are not culturally respectful or transparent.
mitigation_strategy: Establish early partnerships with trusted local artisan collectives and secure mineral access through pre-negotiated agreements; conduct co-creation workshops with community leaders to ensure cultural sensitivity and build trust before material procurement begins.

delay_risks: Delays in finalizing partnership agreements with Greenlandic artisan collectives due to bureaucratic processes, lack of formal documentation, or misalignment in expectations regarding intellectual property and compensation.
mitigation_strategy: Initiate outreach and legal alignment discussions early using bilingual (Danish/Greenlandic) contracts and involve the advisory council as mediators; schedule regular check-ins and use a shared digital platform for transparent progress tracking and feedback.

delay_risks: Procurement delays due to customs clearance issues, shipping disruptions from Arctic weather, or failure to meet regulatory requirements for imported materials; risk of non-compliance with environmental or safety standards if sourcing is not properly vetted.
mitigation_strategy: Pre-approve all material suppliers through the advisory council and compliance team; utilize emergency air freight agreements already secured; conduct pre-shipment inspections and maintain full documentation to ensure rapid customs processing and regulatory compliance.

delay_risks: Seasonal demand fluctuations may lead to underutilization or overcapacity in programming, especially if winter programs fail to attract sufficient participation. Delays in securing partnerships with hotel providers could delay the rollout of tourist packages.
mitigation_strategy: Conduct pre-season demand forecasting using historical data and community surveys; pilot-test themed winter programs with small groups before full launch. Establish early contracts with at least three hotel partners to secure distribution channels for tourist packages.

delay_risks: Winter weather conditions in Nuuk may limit public attendance, and local interest in indoor cultural activities might be lower than expected during peak winter months. Scheduling conflicts with other community events could reduce turnout.
mitigation_strategy: Design 'Winter Pottery Nights' with flexible timing (evening and weekend slots) and promote through targeted outreach via schools, elders’ council, and social media. Offer incentives such as free materials or raffle prizes to boost initial participation.

delay_risks: Summer tourism peaks may overwhelm staff capacity if micro-courses are not scaled appropriately. Tourist demand may be unpredictable due to fluctuating travel patterns or external factors like flight disruptions.
mitigation_strategy: Develop modular, short-duration micro-courses (1–2 hours) that can be delivered by rotating instructors. Partner with hotels to co-promote packages and implement a real-time booking system with automated capacity alerts to prevent overbooking.

delay_risks: Delays in securing artist availability due to competing seasonal commitments or travel restrictions during winter months; potential for misalignment between artist vision and workshop programming needs.
mitigation_strategy: Establish a pre-approved roster of rotating artists with flexible scheduling, secure commitments through formal contracts with advance deposits, and integrate feedback loops early in the planning phase to align expectations.

delay_risks: Inaccurate demand forecasting due to limited historical data on seasonal attendance patterns; unexpected shifts in tourism trends or local participation rates could lead to underutilization or overcrowding.
mitigation_strategy: Leverage initial soft-launch data and real-time tracking dashboards to refine forecasts iteratively; conduct monthly review cycles with stakeholder input to adjust programming based on actual utilization and feedback.

delay_risks: Delayed or inconsistent data collection from sensors and user feedback systems may hinder accurate performance evaluation; low response rates from community surveys could skew results.
mitigation_strategy: Automate data collection via IoT sensors and digital dashboards to ensure continuous monitoring; incentivize survey participation with small community rewards and distribute bilingual forms through trusted local networks to boost engagement.

delay_risks: Delays in securing community buy-in or participation due to cultural sensitivities, misaligned expectations, or low turnout for co-creation events. Potential for inconsistent feedback from youth apprenticeships if school partnerships are delayed.
mitigation_strategy: Engage elders and artists early through advisory council meetings; host pilot events with small, diverse groups to test engagement models; establish clear communication channels via bi-monthly surveys and public dashboards to maintain transparency and responsiveness.

delay_risks: Potential delays in defining membership tiers due to unclear pricing strategy or lack of resident feedback. Risk of low uptake if the value proposition is not clearly communicated or perceived as exclusive.
mitigation_strategy: Conduct pre-launch focus groups with local residents to validate pricing tiers and benefits; use real-time survey data to refine the offering; launch a soft-tiered trial version with flexible access to gather early adoption insights.

delay_risks: Delays in finalizing package content due to coordination challenges with hotel partners or last-minute changes in tourism demand forecasts. Risk of mismatched branding or delivery issues if contracts are not aligned.
mitigation_strategy: Establish a joint task force with key hotel partners to co-develop packages; set fixed milestones for content approval and contract signing; use existing seasonal programming data to align offerings with peak tourist periods.

delay_risks: Potential low uptake due to perceived lack of value or financial uncertainty among residents; technical delays in integrating flexible pricing into the booking system; inconsistent enforcement across staff leading to revenue leakage.
mitigation_strategy: Conduct pilot testing with a small group of community members to refine pricing thresholds and messaging; assign a dedicated tech lead to oversee integration with the online booking platform; provide clear training and standardized guidelines for staff on how to apply the model consistently, including real-time monitoring of participation rates.

delay_risks: Integration complexity due to legacy system limitations; delays from third-party payment gateway compatibility issues; insufficient user testing leading to post-launch bugs or confusion.
mitigation_strategy: Perform a pre-integration audit of the existing booking platform to identify compatibility risks; engage the payment gateway provider early for API support; conduct usability testing with a diverse group of users (including seniors and non-tech-savvy individuals) before launch to ensure clarity and accessibility.

delay_risks: Inconsistent data collection or reporting delays from staff; lack of analytical capacity to interpret trends; slow decision-making due to stakeholder alignment challenges.
mitigation_strategy: Automate data aggregation through the digital dashboard with scheduled weekly exports; assign a rotating revenue analyst role among instructors to ensure consistent oversight; establish a quarterly review meeting with key stakeholders (manager, logistics coordinator, finance liaison) to evaluate performance and approve adjustments promptly.

delay_risks: Delays in securing community participation due to low awareness or mistrust, potential for cultural misalignment in co-created content, and challenges in coordinating schedules across diverse stakeholders including elders, artists, and youth groups.
mitigation_strategy: Proactively engage community leaders through culturally respectful outreach; host inclusive, transparent workshops with clear agendas and feedback mechanisms; use bilingual communication tools and involve youth apprentices as peer ambassadors to build trust and ensure representation.

delay_risks: Low turnout due to lack of local interest or perceived exclusion, logistical challenges in organizing public installations, and potential delays in material sourcing or weather-related disruptions during outdoor events.
mitigation_strategy: Promote the event through targeted community channels (schools, radio, social media in Greenlandic), partner with local influencers and youth groups, pre-test installation designs with community focus groups, and schedule the event during favorable weather windows with contingency plans for indoor alternatives.

delay_risks: Limited student enrollment due to lack of school buy-in, scheduling conflicts with school curricula, or insufficient mentorship capacity among instructors, which could delay program launch and reduce impact.
mitigation_strategy: Establish formal agreements with schools early in the process, align apprenticeship timelines with academic calendars, provide incentives for teacher involvement, and implement a structured onboarding and mentoring framework with rotating instructor support to ensure consistent guidance.

delay_risks: Delays may occur due to technical integration challenges with AI tools, lack of local cultural data for training models, or resistance from traditional artists wary of technology replacing handcraft. Additionally, software licensing or compatibility issues could slow deployment.
mitigation_strategy: Collaborate with local artists and elders during the design phase to co-create culturally relevant training datasets; pilot the tool with a small group of instructors before full rollout; secure software licenses in advance and conduct compatibility testing with existing systems; provide hands-on workshops to build trust and familiarity among staff.

delay_risks: Low response rates from community members due to language barriers, digital access limitations, or perceived lack of impact from past feedback. Delays may also arise if survey tools are not available in both Danish and Greenlandic or if translation errors affect comprehension.
mitigation_strategy: Distribute surveys via multiple channels (in-person at events, printed materials, SMS, and bilingual digital forms); offer small incentives (e.g., free workshop vouchers) for participation; partner with local schools and youth groups to promote engagement; use pre-tested, culturally appropriate language and visuals; conduct bi-monthly review meetings to demonstrate how feedback leads to real changes.

delay_risks: Delays may result from incomplete artwork approvals due to unresolved cultural sensitivities, technical issues with digital publishing platforms, or delays in printing and installation logistics—especially during winter months when transport is unreliable.
mitigation_strategy: Establish a clear approval workflow involving the advisory council before finalizing artworks; create a digital archive with version control and metadata; schedule physical installations during spring/summer months when transport is more reliable; maintain backup printing contracts with local providers and use weather-resilient mounting solutions for outdoor displays.

delay_risks: Delays in securing final approvals from Nuuk Municipality or unexpected regulatory feedback could push back the completion of pre-opening milestones. Additionally, last-minute changes in lease terms or facility inspection results may require rework.
mitigation_strategy: Begin all permit and compliance applications early with bilingual documentation; maintain a buffer of at least two weeks in the timeline for review cycles; assign a dedicated liaison to coordinate with Nuuk Municipality and ensure timely responses.

delay_risks: The certified Arctic building specialist may be unavailable due to high demand during winter months, or unforeseen structural issues discovered during the audit could require redesigns. Delays in scheduling or access to the site would also impact progress.
mitigation_strategy: Book the certified Arctic specialist at least six weeks in advance; conduct preliminary site assessments before the audit to identify potential red flags; maintain a contingency plan with a secondary qualified auditor on standby.

delay_risks: The Building Code Compliance Certificate or Fire Safety Certification may be delayed due to incomplete documentation, missing inspections, or non-compliance with Arctic-specific safety standards. Equipment or design flaws discovered late could require costly revisions.
mitigation_strategy: Prepare all required documentation in both Danish and Greenlandic well in advance; conduct internal mock inspections using checklists aligned with NFPA and Bygningsreglementet; engage the certification bodies early for pre-submission feedback to avoid rejections.

delay_risks: Delays in obtaining the Building Code Compliance Certificate and Fire Safety Certification may occur due to incomplete documentation, missing inspections, or regulatory backlogs from Nuuk Municipality, especially during peak review periods.
mitigation_strategy: Submit all required documentation in both Danish and Greenlandic well in advance (by April 20, 2026), conduct a pre-submission review with the Katuaq Cultural Centre Oversight Committee, and schedule follow-up meetings with Nuuk Municipality planning officials to track progress and resolve issues proactively.

delay_risks: Incomplete or inaccurate records of safety training, liability waivers, and incident logs could delay compliance verification, particularly if staff fail to submit documentation on time or if language barriers hinder recordkeeping.
mitigation_strategy: Implement a centralized digital tracking system with automated reminders for staff to log training and waiver completion; conduct bi-weekly audits of records and provide bilingual support for documentation; assign the Safety Officer to oversee compliance and report directly to the Clay Workshop Manager.

delay_risks: Failure to maintain up-to-date records of safety training, liability waivers, and incident logs—due to staff turnover, poor coordination, or lack of accountability—could result in non-compliance during the final audit, leading to delays in opening.
mitigation_strategy: Integrate record maintenance into onboarding and monthly performance reviews; use the Digital Shift Calendar to flag upcoming training deadlines; designate a rotating compliance champion among instructors to ensure consistent oversight and timely submissions.

delay_risks: Delays in securing final approvals from Nuuk Municipality, unexpected issues during fit-out requiring rework, or last-minute changes in staffing or equipment delivery could push back the pre-opening milestones. Additionally, unforeseen regulatory hurdles or compliance gaps may require additional time to resolve.
mitigation_strategy: Proactively coordinate with Nuuk Municipality and regulatory bodies through scheduled check-ins; conduct a dry run of all compliance documentation prior to submission; maintain a buffer of 10 days in the timeline for unexpected delays; and assign a dedicated compliance liaison to monitor progress and escalate issues early.

delay_risks: Lease negotiations may stall due to landlord availability, legal review delays, or disagreements over terms such as rent escalation clauses or use restrictions. Winter conditions could also delay site access or inspections needed for lease finalization.
mitigation_strategy: Engage a local real estate advisor familiar with Nuuk commercial leases to expedite negotiations; prepare a standardized lease agreement draft in both Danish and Greenlandic for faster review; schedule site visits and legal reviews in parallel; and include a contingency clause allowing for temporary space use if needed while finalizing the primary lease.

delay_risks: Instructor hiring may be delayed due to limited local talent pool, lack of qualified applicants, or extended onboarding timelines. Language barriers or cultural integration challenges could also slow down effective team integration.
mitigation_strategy: Launch a targeted recruitment campaign in collaboration with local schools, Katuaq Cultural Centre, and tourism networks; offer competitive part-time compensation and flexible scheduling; implement a structured onboarding program with bilingual training materials and peer mentorship; and begin hiring 4 weeks before the target start date to allow for overlap and adjustment.

delay_risks: Delays in delivery or installation of kilns, worktables, drying racks, and ventilation systems due to Arctic shipping disruptions, supplier backlogs, or unforeseen logistical challenges during winter months. Additionally, potential for design changes or safety compliance issues requiring rework.
mitigation_strategy: Pre-order all major equipment with guaranteed delivery timelines; maintain a buffer of 10 days in the schedule; conduct pre-installation inspections with certified Arctic contractors; prioritize modular, pre-fabricated components to reduce on-site assembly time.

delay_risks: First course delivery may be delayed due to last-minute instructor availability, incomplete training, or unresolved supply chain issues (e.g., clay shortages). Weather-related access problems to the workshop could also impact the soft launch date.
mitigation_strategy: Implement a cross-trained instructor rotation with digital shift calendar tracking; ensure all instructors complete mandatory safety and curriculum training at least two weeks prior; confirm clay buffer stock is on-site and verified before launch; conduct a dry-run session one week before August 1 to test logistics and staffing coverage.

delay_risks: Session cancellations may occur due to unexpected staff absences (illness, travel delays), material shortages despite buffer stock, or last-minute equipment failures not caught during fit-out. Volunteer liability issues could also lead to operational halts if waivers are incomplete.
mitigation_strategy: Enforce a 100% completion of liability waivers and safety training for all staff and volunteers before any session; implement real-time monitoring via IoT sensors and digital dashboard to preempt supply or environmental issues; maintain a rotating backup instructor roster and use the digital shift calendar to auto-reassign sessions in case of absence.
